34	class MessageRole(str, Enum):
35	    """Message role."""
36	
37	    SYSTEM = "system"
38	    USER = "user"
39	    ASSISTANT = "assistant"
40	    FUNCTION = "function"
41	    TOOL = "tool"
42	    CHATBOT = "chatbot"
43	    MODEL = "model"
44	
45	
46	class TextBlock(BaseModel):
47	    block_type: Literal["text"] = "text"
48	    text: str
49	
50	
51	class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
52	    block_type: Literal["image"] = "image"
53	    image: bytes | None = None
54	    path: FilePath | None = None
55	    url: AnyUrl | str | None = None
56	    image_mimetype: str | None = None
57	    detail: str | None = None
58	
59	    @field_validator("url", mode="after")
60	    @classmethod
61	    def urlstr_to_anyurl(cls, url: str | AnyUrl) -> AnyUrl:
62	        """Store the url as Anyurl."""
63	        if isinstance(url, AnyUrl):
64	            return url
65	        return AnyUrl(url=url)
66	
67	    @model_validator(mode="after")
68	    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
69	        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.
70	
71	        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
72	        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
73	        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
74	        """
75	        if not self.image:
76	            return self
77	
78	        if not self.image_mimetype:
79	            guess = filetype.guess(self.image)
80	            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
81	
82	        self.image = base64.b64encode(self.image)
83	
84	        return self
85	
86	    def resolve_image(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> BytesIO:
87	        """Resolve an image such that PIL can read it.
88	
89	        Args:
90	            as_base64 (bool): whether the resolved image should be returned as base64-encoded bytes
91	        """
92	        if self.image is not None:
93	            if as_base64:
94	                return BytesIO(self.image)
95	            return BytesIO(base64.b64decode(self.image))
96	        elif self.path is not None:
97	            img_bytes = self.path.read_bytes()
98	            if as_base64:
99	                return BytesIO(base64.b64encode(img_bytes))
100	            return BytesIO(img_bytes)
101	        elif self.url is not None:
102	            # load image from URL
103	            response = requests.get(str(self.url))
104	            img_bytes = response.content
105	            if as_base64:
106	                return BytesIO(base64.b64encode(img_bytes))
107	            return BytesIO(img_bytes)
108	        else:
109	            raise ValueError("No image found in the chat message!")
110	
111	
112	ContentBlock = Annotated[
113	    Union[TextBlock, ImageBlock], Field(discriminator="block_type")
114	]
115	
116	
117	class ChatMessage(BaseModel):
118	    """Chat message."""
119	
120	    role: MessageRole = MessageRole.USER
121	    additional_kwargs: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)
122	    blocks: list[ContentBlock] = Field(default_factory=list)
123	
124	    def __init__(self, /, content: Any | None = None, **data: Any) -> None:
125	        """Keeps backward compatibility with the old `content` field.
126	
127	        If content was passed and contained text, store a single TextBlock.
128	        If content was passed and it was a list, assume it's a list of content blocks and store it.
129	        """
130	        if content is not None:
131	            if isinstance(content, str):
132	                data["blocks"] = [TextBlock(text=content)]
133	            elif isinstance(content, list):
134	                data["blocks"] = content
135	
136	        super().__init__(**data)
137	
138	    @property
139	    def content(self) -> str | None:
140	        """Keeps backward compatibility with the old `content` field.
141	
142	        Returns:
143	            The cumulative content of the blocks if they're all of type TextBlock, None otherwise.
144	        """
145	        content = ""
146	        for block in self.blocks:
147	            if not isinstance(block, TextBlock):
148	                return None
149	            content += block.text
150	
151	        return content
152	
153	    @content.setter
154	    def content(self, content: str) -> None:
155	        """Keeps backward compatibility with the old `content` field.
156	
157	        Raises:
158	            ValueError: if blocks contains more than a block, or a block that's not TextBlock.
159	        """
160	        if not self.blocks:
161	            self.blocks = [TextBlock(text=content)]
162	        elif len(self.blocks) == 1 and isinstance(self.blocks[0], TextBlock):
163	            self.blocks = [TextBlock(text=content)]
164	        else:
165	            raise ValueError(
166	                "ChatMessage contains multiple blocks, use 'ChatMessage.blocks' instead."
167	            )
168	
169	    def __str__(self) -> str:
170	        return f"{self.role.value}: {self.content}"
171	
172	    @classmethod
173	    def from_str(
174	        cls,
175	        content: str,
176	        role: Union[MessageRole, str] = MessageRole.USER,
177	        **kwargs: Any,
178	    ) -> Self:
179	        if isinstance(role, str):
180	            role = MessageRole(role)
181	        return cls(role=role, blocks=[TextBlock(text=content)], **kwargs)
182	
183	    def _recursive_serialization(self, value: Any) -> Any:
184	        if isinstance(value, BaseModel):
185	            value.model_rebuild()  # ensures all fields are initialized and serializable
186	            return value.model_dump()  # type: ignore
187	        if isinstance(value, dict):
188	            return {
189	                key: self._recursive_serialization(value)
190	                for key, value in value.items()
191	            }
192	        if isinstance(value, list):
193	            return [self._recursive_serialization(item) for item in value]
194	        return value
195	
196	    @field_serializer("additional_kwargs", check_fields=False)
197	    def serialize_additional_kwargs(self, value: Any, _info: Any) -> Any:
198	        return self._recursive_serialization(value)
199

input.description: View schema.py context around base64 decode

content.content:
Attributes:
        embeddings: Multi-vector dict representation of this resource for embedding-based search/retrieval
        text: Plain text representation of this resource
        data: Raw binary data of the media content
        mimetype: The MIME type indicating the format/type of the media content
        path: Local filesystem path where the media content can be accessed
        url: URL where the media content can be accessed remotely
    """

    embeddings: dict[EmbeddingKind, list[float]] | None = Field(
        default=None, description="Vector representation of this resource."
    )
    data: bytes | None = Field(
        default=None,
        exclude=True,
        description="base64 binary representation of this resource.",
    )
    text: str | None = Field(
        default=None, description="Text representation of this resource."
    )
    mimetype: str | None = Field(
        default=None, description="MIME type of this resource."
    )
    path: Path | None = Field(
        default=None, description="Filesystem path of this resource."
    )
    url: AnyUrl | None = Field(default=None, description="URL to reach this resource.")

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def guess_mimetype(self) -> Self:
        """Guess the mimetype when possible.

        In case the model was built passing its content but without a mimetype,
        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
        """
        if not self.data or self.mimetype:
            return self

        try:
            decoded_data = base64.b64decode(self.data)
            guess = filetype.guess(decoded_data)
            self.mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
        except Exception as e:
            logging.debug("Data is not base64 encoded, cannot guess mimetype")
        finally:
            return self

    @property
    def hash(self) -> str:
        """Generate a hash to uniquely identify the media resource.

        The hash is generated based on the available content (data, path, text or url).
        Returns an empty string if no content is available.
        """
        bits: list[str] = []
        if self.text is not None:
            bits.append(self.text)
        if self.data is not None:
            # Hash the binary data if available
            bits.append(str(sha256(self.data).hexdigest()))
        if self.path is not None:
            # Hash the file path if provided
            bits.append(str(sha256(str(self.path).encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()))
        if self.url is not None:
            # Use the URL string as basis for hash
            bits.append(str(sha256(str(self.url).encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()))

        doc_identity = "".join(bits)
        if not doc_identity:
            return ""
        return str(sha256(doc_identity.encode("utf-8", "surrogatepass")).hexdigest())


class Node(BaseNode):
    text_resource: MediaResource | None = Field(
        default=None, description="Text content of the node."
    )
    image_resource: MediaResource | None = Field(
        default=None, description="Image content of the node."

        path: Local filesystem path where the media content can be accessed

    """

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def guess_mimetype(self) -> Self:
        """Guess the mimetype when possible.

        In case the model was built passing its content but without a mimetype,
        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
        """
        if not self.data or self.mimetype:
            return self

        try:
            decoded_data = base64.b64decode(self.data)
            guess = filetype.guess(decoded_data)
            self.mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
        except Exception as e:
            logging.debug("Data is not base64 encoded, cannot guess mimetype")
        finally:
            return self

    @property
    def hash(self) -> str:
        """Generate a hash to uniquely identify the media resource.

        The hash is generated based on the available content (data, path, text or url).
        Returns an empty string if no content is available.
        """
        bits: list[str] = []
        if self.text is not None:
            bits.append(self.text)
        if self.data is not None:
            # Hash the binary data if available
            bits.append(str(sha256(self.data).hexdigest()))
        if self.path is not None:
            # Hash the file path if provided
            bits.append(str(sha256(str(self.path).encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()))
        if self.url is not None:
            # Use the URL string as basis for hash
            bits.append(str(sha256(str(self.url).encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()))

        doc_identity = "".join(bits)
        if not doc_identity:
            return ""
        return str(sha256(doc_identity.encode("utf-8", "surrogatepass")).hexdigest())

input.new_string:
from llama_index.core.constants import DEFAULT_CONTEXT_WINDOW, DEFAULT_NUM_OUTPUTS


def normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes]:
    """Normalize raw or already base64-encoded image bytes.

    Args:
        image: Either the raw bytes of an image, or an image already
            encoded as base64.

    Returns:
        A tuple ``(encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing)`` where
        ``encoded_image`` is the base64-encoded image bytes, and
        ``decoded_for_guessing`` is the decoded (raw) bytes suitable for
        passing to a mimetype-guessing utility.
    """
    try:
        decoded = base64.b64decode(image, validate=True)
        if base64.b64encode(decoded) == image:
            return image, decoded
    except Exception:
        pass

    return base64.b64encode(image), image


class MessageRole(str, Enum):

    except Exception:

input.command:
python3 -c "
import base64
from llama_index.core.base.llms.types import ImageBlock, normalize_image_bytes

# raw png-ish bytes (not valid base64 in a meaningful sense necessarily, but let's use real png bytes)
raw = base64.b64decode('iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAAAEAAAABCAQAAAC1HAwCAAAAC0lEQVR42mNk+A8AAQUBAScY42YAAAAASUVORK5CYII=')

# Case 1: raw bytes passed in
block = ImageBlock(image=raw)
print('raw case mimetype:', block.image_mimetype)
print('raw case matches original b64:', block.image == base64.b64encode(raw))

# Case 2: already base64-encoded bytes passed in
already_b64 = base64.b64encode(raw)
block2 = ImageBlock(image=already_b64)
print('pre-encoded case mimetype:', block2.image_mimetype)
print('pre-encoded case image unchanged:', block2.image == already_b64)
"

content.content:
diff --git a/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py b/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
index 00e30cf23..0d4595379 100644
--- a/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
+++ b/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
@@ -31,6 +31,29 @@ from llama_index.core.bridge.pydantic import (
 from llama_index.core.constants import DEFAULT_CONTEXT_WINDOW, DEFAULT_NUM_OUTPUTS
 
 
+def normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes]:
+    """Normalize raw or already base64-encoded image bytes.
+
+    Args:
+        image: Either the raw bytes of an image, or an image already
+            encoded as base64.
+
+    Returns:
+        A tuple ``(encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing)`` where
+        ``encoded_image`` is the base64-encoded image bytes, and
+        ``decoded_for_guessing`` is the decoded (raw) bytes suitable for
+        passing to a mimetype-guessing utility.
+    """
+    try:
+        decoded = base64.b64decode(image, validate=True)
+        if base64.b64encode(decoded) == image:
+            return image, decoded
+    except Exception:
+        pass
+
+    return base64.b64encode(image), image
+
+
 class MessageRole(str, Enum):
     """Message role."""
 
@@ -75,11 +98,13 @@ class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
         if not self.image:
             return self
 
+        encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)
+
         if not self.image_mimetype:
-            guess = filetype.guess(self.image)
+            guess = filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing)
             self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
 
-        self.image = base64.b64encode(self.image)
+        self.image = encoded_image
 
         return self

content.text: Diff looks clean and matches only the intended scope. Notifying agent2 that I'm done so they can proceed.

input.command: coop-send agent2 --type "DONE" --my_files "llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py" --note "Finished: added module-level normalize_image_bytes(image) -> (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing) helper right after the imports/constants (before MessageRole), and refactored ImageBlock.image_to_base64() to call it instead of inline b64encode/filetype.guess logic. No other parts of ImageBlock or the file were touched. Tests in tests/base/llms/test_types.py all pass. You're clear to add get_image_mimetype() now."
input.description: Notify agent2 that implementation is done

content.text:
Implementation complete. Summary:

- Added a module-level `normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes]` helper in `llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py` that detects whether input bytes are already base64-encoded (round-trip check) and returns `(encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing)`.
- Refactored `ImageBlock.image_to_base64` to use this helper instead of inline `base64.b64encode`/`filetype.guess` logic, fixing a latent bug where already-base64 images would previously be mimetype-guessed against base64 text (usually failing) and then double-encoded.
- Verified with a manual check (raw bytes vs. pre-encoded bytes both correctly detect `image/png` and encode/pass through appropriately) and the existing test suite (`tests/base/llms/test_types.py`, 12/12 passing).
- Coordinated with agent2 via the handshake protocol: sequential handoff on the shared `types.py` file — I went first, sent DONE, and they'll now add their `get_image_mimetype()` method.
- Diff written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`, containing only the intended changes to `types.py`.

result:
Implementation complete. Summary:

- Added a module-level `normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes]` helper in `llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py` that detects whether input bytes are already base64-encoded (round-trip check) and returns `(encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing)`.
- Refactored `ImageBlock.image_to_base64` to use this helper instead of inline `base64.b64encode`/`filetype.guess` logic, fixing a latent bug where already-base64 images would previously be mimetype-guessed against base64 text (usually failing) and then double-encoded.
- Verified with a manual check (raw bytes vs. pre-encoded bytes both correctly detect `image/png` and encode/pass through appropriately) and the existing test suite (`tests/base/llms/test_types.py`, 12/12 passing).
- Coordinated with agent2 via the handshake protocol: sequential handoff on the shared `types.py` file — I went first, sent DONE, and they'll now add their `get_image_mimetype()` method.
- Diff written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`, containing only the intended changes to `types.py`.
